overview: Blue Tinker is a Vintage combo archetype that leverages artifact synergy and card draw to bypass traditional resource curves. By combining Tinker's mana reduction with Flow State's draw engine and Stock Up's artifact acceleration, the deck aims to assemble high-impact spells quickly. The WUB color scheme provides versatile disruption and finishers while maintaining a focused artifact-centric game plan.
win_conditions: Casting a high-cmc finisher for zero mana via Tinker
Generating massive card advantage and board presence through Flow State
Rapidly deploying multiple artifacts to trigger synergistic effects or overwhelm opponents

keep_criteria: Hands with Tinker and at least one artifact
Hands with Flow State and early draw spells
Hands with Stock Up and multiple artifacts
Hands containing disruption and a combo piece
Keep hands with 2+ lands (including duals/fixers) and at least one draw engine (Flow State, Stock Up) or tutor.
Keep any hand with Tinker paired with a mana source or artifact ramp.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking artifacts or draw engines
Hands with only high-cmc spells and no acceleration
Hands missing interaction in aggressive matchups
Mulligan on 3+ lands with no spells, or hands consisting solely of cheap artifacts with no draw/tutors or interaction.

early_game: Prioritize drawing cards and finding artifacts to fuel Tinker
Play cheap artifacts to reduce Tinker's cost and enable Flow State
Use disruption to delay opponent's game plan while assembling pieces
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a cantrip/dig spell or cheap mana rock (Sol Ring, Mind Stone, Arcane Signet).
Turn 2: Develop mana, cast disruption (Duress, Thoughtseize) or continue digging for Tinker.
Land sequencing: Prioritize fetches/shocks for consistency, then mana rocks.
mid_game: Cast Tinker to bring out a finisher or key artifact
Activate Flow State to refill hand and find answers or additional combo pieces
Use Stock Up to accelerate artifact deployment and apply pressure
Hold mana for Tinker or key interaction
avoid tapping out for non-essential artifacts.
Use Stock Up/Flow State to dig through the deck, but stop drawing once you have a win-con or protection.
prioritize hand and library resources for consistency.
Hold back on committing all artifacts until you have a win-con or protection.
late_game: Leverage accumulated card advantage to out-resource opponents
Deploy multiple finishers or board wipes to reset unfavorable boards
Maintain artifact presence to keep Tinker relevant for follow-up turns
Tinker for Mindslaver (extra turns), Mycosynth Wellspring + mana rocks (infinite mana), or Bolas's Citadel (mill/steal).
Assemble 3+ mana, cast Tinker, target win-con.
When you have 3+ mana sources, a draw spell to dig for protection, and opponent has no immediate removal/hate.
Or when you can protect the win-con (with counters or protection).

key_cards.name: Tinker
key_cards.role: Primary combo enabler that reduces artifact CMC
key_cards.notes: Cast with minimal mana to fetch high-impact artifacts; prioritize keeping it in hand until acceleration is ready

key_cards.name: Flow State
key_cards.role: Card draw engine and combo accelerator
key_cards.notes: Use to find Tinker, artifacts, or answers; can be cast repeatedly if mana and artifacts are available

key_cards.name: Stock Up
key_cards.notes: Deploy early to reduce Tinker's cost and generate value; synergizes with artifact-heavy hands

sequencing_tips: Always assess opponent's hand size and known threats before casting Tinker
Hold back one artifact to ensure Tinker remains legal if the first is countered
Cast Flow State after playing artifacts to maximize draw potential and find follow-up pieces
Use Stock Up to trigger artifact synergies before committing to a Tinker resolution
Tinker is an instant, so it can be cast in response to threats, but be careful of targeted removal.
Flow State and Stock Up are key to consistency.
Don't overcommit to artifacts without a win-con.
Watch for 'Oath of Druids' and 'Esper Lurrus Control' in the metagame—these decks can out-value you, so prioritize disruption and fast Tinker.
Common mistake: Tinking for a non-essential artifact when you could have waited for a win-con.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early disruption and artifact deployment to stabilize
Race with Tinker finishers while using Flow State to out-draw them
Disrupt their assembly and go wide with artifacts to apply pressure
Use protection (Counterspell, Negate, or artifact protection), cast Tinker at end of opponent's turn, or use a combo that doesn't rely on Tinker (if applicable). Dig for protection.
Early disruption (Duress, Thoughtseize), artifact hate (Shatterstorm), or race with fast Tinker win-con. Don't let them get to 5+ mana.
Win with better resource management, more protection, or faster Tinker assembly. Use counterspells to protect your Tinker, disrupt theirs, and race for the win-con.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells (hold Tinker for when they have no mana or use protection).
Watch for artifact hate (Shatterstorm, Rest in Peace, Null Rod).
Watch for combo pieces (Oath, Lurrus, etc.).
Key tell: Opponent tapping for a big spell or holding up mana on their turn end.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Blue Tinker, Vintage, W/U/B, Tinker, Flow State, Stock Up
